channel.title: Energy & Environment
channel.description: News, Analysis and Opinion from POLITICO

item.title: 'A bit of FOMO': Oil companies give Venezuela a second look amid Mideast chaos
item.description: President Donald Trump’s policy misfires in the Middle East are having one unexpected beneficiary — his policy goals in Venezuela.

media:content.media:title: Venezuelan Vice President Delcy Rodriguez takes part in talks with the Russian foreign minister in Moscow on March 1, 2019.

item.dc:creator: By Ben Lefebvre and James Bikales
item.dc:contributor: By Ben Lefebvre and James Bikales

item.title: Trump is urging tankers to sail through Hormuz. Vessels aren’t so sure yet.
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said ship traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has already increased since a peace deal was announced, but industry associations are urging caution.

media:content.media:title: Iran’s state-linked Tasnim News Agency said that Iran intends to start charging ships for services after the 60-day period for technical negotiations around the deal.

item.dc:creator: By Mike Soraghan, Martina Sapio and Carlos Anchondo
item.dc:contributor: By Mike Soraghan, Martina Sapio and Carlos Anchondo

item.title: Diesel mechanics queue up for Trump pardons
item.description: President Donald Trump pardoned a mechanic who disabled pollution control monitors on hundreds of diesel trucks. Now others are lobbying for similar clemency.

media:content.media:title: A growing number of people convicted of crimes under the Clean Air Act, particularly diesel mechanics who tampered with emissions controls, are seeking pardons from President Donald Trump.

item.dc:creator: By Alex Guillén

item.title: Trump’s massive arch could snarl traffic for years
item.description: The president’s proposed 250-foot-tall monument is facing criticism from lawmakers and Vietnam veterans.

media:content.media:credit: Harrison Design/U.S. Commission of Fine Arts
media:content.media:title: A rendering of President Donald Trump's planned triumphal arch on April 10.

item.dc:creator: By Heather Richards

item.title: Algae resurfaces in reflecting pool after multimillion-dollar fixes
item.description: The Interior Department said the algae is residual after supply lines sat dormant for weeks.

media:content.media:title: Green algae can be seen Friday in the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool.

item.dc:creator: By Kinnia Cheuk

item.title: US military escorting growing number of oil tankers through Hormuz, Energy secretary says
item.description: The increased traffic through the Strait of Hormuz comes as Trump and Iran continue haggling over a peace deal.

media:content.media:title: Energy Secretary Chris Wright testifies before the House Energy and Commerce Subcommittee on Energy on Capitol Hill on April 16.

item.dc:creator: By Mike Lee

item.title: ‘This is not normal’: Trump leans on MAGA organizer to revive coal
item.description: The Energy Department is giving millions to a company partially led by a far-right activist and telecom executive to build the nation’s first coal-fired power plant since 2013.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump's hopes of a U.S. coal renaissance relies in part on a company that wants to build a coal plant and carbon capture project in West Virginia.

item.dc:creator: By Zack Colman, Ian M. Stevenson and Hannah Northey
item.dc:contributor: By Zack Colman, Ian M. Stevenson and Hannah Northey

item.title: Inside the campaign to discredit a key climate science report
item.description: An emerging field of research that can measure how much climate change has worsened individual disasters is under attack by friends of the fossil fuel industry. Billions of dollars are at stake.

media:content.media:title: The National Academy of Sciences building is seen in Washington on May 1.

item.dc:creator: By Corbin Hiar, Lesley Clark and Chelsea Harvey
item.dc:contributor: By Corbin Hiar, Lesley Clark and Chelsea Harvey

item.title: EPA won't set nationwide standards for data centers
item.description: "EPA is not the party that is negotiating and or mediating or refereeing that deal," the EPA administrator said.

media:content.media:description: Zeldin addresses data center skepticism: 'It really depends on how the deal is structured'
media:content.media:title: Zeldin addresses data center skepticism: 'It really depends on how the deal is structured'

item.dc:creator: By Alex Guillén

item.title: Trump attacks on renewables ‘toxic’ to permitting talks
item.description: A top Democrat said Wednesday he wanted to see more movement on renewable energy approvals. A White House official didn’t appear willing to budge.

media:content.media:description: Inside the push for permitting reform at POLITICO's Energy Summit
media:content.media:title: Inside the push for permitting reform at POLITICO's Energy Summit

item.dc:creator: By Timothy Cama

item.title: Trump administration in 'active dialogue' on strategic petroleum reserve in California
item.description: The plan would almost certainly run into opposition from Democratic Gov. Gavin Newsom.

media:content.media:title: Energy Secretary Chris Wright speaks in front of Sable Offshore Corporation's oil processing facility in Santa Barbara County, California, on Friday.

item.dc:creator: By Noah Baustin and Ben Lefebvre
item.dc:contributor: By Noah Baustin and Ben Lefebvre

item.title: Republicans probe China's influence in data center opposition
item.description: The House GOP is asking the administration to investigate whether China-linked entities are fueling resistance to artificial intelligence infrastructure.

media:content.media:title: House Energy and Commerce Chair Brett Guthrie (R-Ky.) speaking with reporters at the Capitol.

item.dc:creator: By Nico Portuondo

item.title: Oil industry warns Trump administration of price spikes within weeks
item.description: Industry executives said the loss of oil through the Strait of Hormuz is draining petroleum inventories to dangerously low levels.

media:content.media:title: A bulk cargo ship sits at anchor in the Strait of Hormuz off Bandar Abbas, Iran, on May 2.

item.dc:creator: By Ben Lefebvre and James Bikales
item.dc:contributor: By Ben Lefebvre and James Bikales

item.title: A judge said the Trump administration can’t dismantle a weather research center. The damage may already be done.
item.description: The National Center for Atmospheric Research is the latest example of how the Trump administration’s efforts to chainsaw the federal government can happen too fast for the courts or Congress to counter.

media:content.media:title: Office of Management and Budget Director Russell Vought prepares to testify at a House Budget Committee hearing on Capitol Hill on April 15.

item.dc:creator: By Scott Waldman

item.title: Unions flex muscle as states weigh data center rules
item.description: Labor unions are using their political power — and a jobs message — to influence state efforts to regulate data centers.

media:content.media:title: Workers examine equipment during the construction of a bitcoin data center in Virginia Beach, Virginia, on Feb. 9, 2018.

item.dc:creator: By Jason Plautz

item.title: Facing a Western water crisis, Trump turns to Democrats’ climate law
item.description: As Colorado River water levels reach disastrous lows, the administration looks to draw cash from the 2022 law it sought to neuter.

media:content.media:title: Farmer Larry Cox walks in a plowed field with his dog, Brodie, at his farm on Aug. 15, 2022, near Brawley, California.

item.dc:creator: By Annie Snider

item.title: ‘Holding our breath’: Hurricane season is here, and FEMA is shorthanded
item.description: The agency’s workforce has shrunk by almost 20 percent under President Donald Trump.

media:content.media:title: Members of the FEMA Urban Search and Rescue Task Force look for survivors near Asheville, North Carolina, in the aftermath of Hurricane Helene in 2024.

item.dc:creator: By Thomas Frank

item.title: AI companies want power fast. The electric grid's gatekeeper wants them to learn the rules.
item.description: The tech industry has shown a Trump-era command of Washington politics. But it's had a steeper learning curve mastering the rules that govern the U.S. electric grid.

media:content.media:title: Google, OpenAI, Microsoft and other tech giants are racing to have their views heard in Washington on energy and data centers.

item.dc:creator: By Kelsey Tamborrino and Joel Kirkland
item.dc:contributor: By Kelsey Tamborrino and Joel Kirkland

item.title: Culture warrior leads incumbent in GOP runoff for Texas oil regulator
item.description: Republican leaders have blasted Bo French for past comments about minority groups. Now, he may be headed to a general election in a bid to oversee oil and gas.

media:content.media:title: Bo French is a Republican candidate for a spot on the Railroad Commission of Texas.

item.dc:creator: By Mike Lee

item.title: War-driven gas spike puts pressure on summer travelers — and Republicans
item.description: Memorial Day marks the traditional start of summer driving season — with an added dollop of political angst for President Donald Trump's party.

media:content.media:credit: Frederic J. Brown/AFP via Getty Images
media:content.media:title: Gas prices above $6 per gallon are displayed at Chevron and Shell stations in Monterey Park, California, April 30, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By James Bikales and Mike Soraghan
item.dc:contributor: By James Bikales and Mike Soraghan

item.title: Trump punts climate rule in hopes of lowering food prices
item.description: The president postponed requirements forcing grocery stores to buy climate-friendly refrigeration, a nod to rising costs for families.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump is joined by grocery chain owners and politicians as he speaks in the Oval Office of the White House on Thursday in Washington. Trump announced an extension of Biden-era EPA deadlines for the phase-out of hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs) under the 2023 "technology transfer” rule, asserting that phasing out deadlines and exempting road refrigeration equipment would lower grocery prices.

item.dc:creator: By Jean Chemnick

item.title: Arts commission approves design for Trump's 250-foot arch
item.description: The Commission of Fine Arts considered some tweaks to the concept for a 250-foot triumphal arch near Arlington National Cemetery.

media:content.media:title: An image and model of President Donald Trump's proposed triumphal arch on April 16 as displayed during a public meeting of the Commission of Fine Arts at the National Building Museum in Washington.

item.dc:creator: By Ian M. Stevenson

item.title: The next Big Oil? Democrats set their sights on utilities.
item.description: Congressional Democrats are increasingly paying attention to electric utilities and the way they’re run as energy prices rise.

media:content.media:title: Rep. Josh Riley (D-N.Y.) this year launched the Congressional Lowering Utility Bills Caucus.

item.dc:creator: By Nico Portuondo and Pavan Acharya
item.dc:contributor: By Nico Portuondo and Pavan Acharya

item.title: Super-sized utility merger runs through Virginia
item.description: Virginia could be a major bump in the road for NextEra's proposed $67 billion merger with Dominion Energy.

media:content.media:title: Democratic Gov. Abigail Spanberger, elected to office in November, ran on holding costs down for households seeing higher utility bills.

item.dc:creator: By Nico Portuondo, Adam Aton and Kelsey Tamborrino
item.dc:contributor: By Nico Portuondo, Adam Aton and Kelsey Tamborrino

item.title: Treasury extends Russian oil sanctions waiver for another month
item.description: A second extension of the waiver indicates that oil market pressures from the closure of the Strait of Hormuz have not eased.

media:content.media:title: The tanker Boracay that allegedly belongs to Russia's so-called shadow fleet, Oct. 2, 2025, off Saint-Nazaire, France's Atlantic coast.

item.dc:creator: By James Bikales

item.title: ‘We’re gonna get sued.’ Texas county passes 1-year data center construction ban.
item.description: The moratorium applies to unincorporated parts of Hill County, a rural area facing eight potential data center developments.

media:content.media:title: Workers prepare to move materials at a power plant that's under construction in East Texas. Hill County, southwest of Dallas, is seeking to control growth related to data centers and the power generation they require.

item.dc:creator: By Mike Lee

item.title: A closely guarded plan to cool Earth is revealed
item.description: A geoengineering company would use tiny specks of silica to block sun rays — and make billions of dollars.

media:content.media:credit: Ye Aung Thu/AFP via Getty Images
media:content.media:title: Stardust Solutions, a geoengineering company, outlined its technologies in six studies that the company plans to have peer-reviewed.

item.dc:creator: By Corbin Hiar

item.title: Iran war is fueling China’s clean energy surge ahead of Trump-Xi talks
item.description: The Iran war is driving countries toward Chinese EVs, solar and batteries — strengthening China's hand as Trump seeks trade wins in Beijing.

media:content.media:title: Chinese Vice President Han Zheng accompanies President Donald Trump as he walks to his motorcade after arriving at Beijing Capital International Airport on Wednesday.

item.dc:creator: By Scott Waldman and Sara Schonhardt
item.dc:contributor: By Scott Waldman and Sara Schonhardt

item.title: 5 things to know about Trump, China and wind power
item.description: The two countries are headed in different directions on energy.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump talks with reporters as he departs the White House for Beijing on Tuesday.

item.dc:creator: By Sara Schonhardt

item.title: ‘The only thing that terrifies me is BYD’: Politicians quake at Chinese EVs
item.description: The question is when, not if, U.S. and European auto markets will open up to Chinese EV investment.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump’s meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping in China has the U.S. auto industry and its backers on edge over market access for Chinese electric vehicles.

item.dc:creator: By Zack Colman, Jordyn Dahl, Sara Schonhardt and Charlie Cooper
item.dc:contributor: By Zack Colman, Jordyn Dahl, Sara Schonhardt and Charlie Cooper
